# junco documentation

> junco is a harness-agnostic task-queue worker that turns Markdown tickets and
> labeled GitHub issues into draft pull requests by driving a coding agent on
> your own machine, against any OpenAI-compatible inference endpoint.

## start

- [Start here](https://junco.ironforgesoftware.com/docs/): What junco is, a sixty-second setup, and a map of these docs.

## reference

- [CLI reference](https://junco.ironforgesoftware.com/docs/cli/): Every junco command and subcommand: synopsis, flags, examples.
- [Configuration](https://junco.ironforgesoftware.com/docs/config/): Every config lever: type, default, live or restart, straight from junco's own registry.
- [Ticket schema](https://junco.ironforgesoftware.com/docs/ticket-schema/): The frontmatter contract, field by field — junco schema rendered as a page.

## field notes

- [Field notes](https://junco.ironforgesoftware.com/docs/field-notes/): Tips, recipes, and answers from running junco in the field.
